statement: The system shall allow each team to manage its own budget independently.
The system shall allow the IFA to control and monitor the budget balance to ensure proper management.
The system shall allow specification of budget policy within the system.
The system shall provide alerts for specified violations.
The system shall allow the financial team to access financial data across teams.
The system shall allow administrative users to view all transaction records.
The system shall allow only IFA administrative personnel and teams to access the budgeting system.
The system shall allow referees to insert game events in real time using a mobile phone.
The system shall make game result data publicly viewable to unregistered guests.
The system shall allow export of data to journalists or press.
The system shall allow team managers and team administrators to view data and perform offline analysis.
The system shall allow only teams to insert budget transactions.
The system shall restrict data access so that each team can only access its own data.
The system shall allow IFA users to view all monetary information, request or be alerted to details, and shall prevent them from modifying monetary data.
The system shall allow definition of scheduling policies.
The system shall allocate referees to games based on defined policies, ensuring that the same referee is not assigned to both teams in a match.
The system shall enable referees to set preferences such as vacation and consider those preferences when scheduling.
The system shall allow manual adjustment of the schedule.
The system shall support schedule overrides by requiring a rationale explanation and confirmation from a second IFA representative.
The system shall enforce minimum gap days between matches as defined in the policy.
The system shall allow fans to subscribe to games or players and receive push notifications when events occur.

The system shall provide a web‑based application for teams and IFA administrators, mobile applications for referees and fans, and deliver notifications exclusively within the applications without using email.
The system shall provide a unified system with distinct interfaces for each stakeholder role.
The system shall allow only referees and official channels to update game data, ensuring that all game data originates exclusively from these sources.
The system shall send automatic reminder notifications, such as a reminder five minutes before a game starts and reminders for report submissions.
The system shall support up to 50,000 concurrent users.
The system shall provide response times of one to two seconds for fans and administrators, and real‑time high‑priority responses for referees.
The system shall be hosted and all storage and computing resources shall be provided by an external cloud provider.
The system shall support localization of language, currency, and time zone, and shall comply with local regulations.
The system shall encrypt budget data to prevent leakage.
The system shall allow players to update their social network page within the portal.
The system shall require each user to have a profile that includes a username.
The system shall support a workflow in which a team manager requests a new team, IFA approves the registration, and thereafter the team can manage its resources.
The system shall retain all past scheduling archives.
The system shall allow the IFA to define leagues and seasons, appoint referees, and set budget and scheduling policies.
